<!-- Generated by /make-a-plan-lite — quick vertical slices with EARS acceptance criteria -->

# Plan: Lecturer Progress Tracking

> Source: Conversation request — lecturer needs to track which talking points already covered during a live lecture, and remember last touched popup.

## Technical Approach

New `ProgressContext` mirrors existing `TimerContext` pattern: React context + `localStorage` persistence + reset operations at three scopes (section / module / all). Each talking point gets a stable path-based id of the form `<moduleId>::<sectionId>::<index>[.<index>...]` derived during render — no data file migration. The leading bullet `●` becomes a clickable button that toggles a `covered` flag with visual feedback (opacity dim + ✓ glyph). The `DetailPopup` gets a new primary "Готово" action button — only this button marks the point covered AND records it as `lastOpened`; closing via X / Esc / backdrop click does nothing to progress. `lastOpened` gives a single subtle visual accent (left border) on the most recently confirmed point, answering "where was I?". Reset controls live in `TimerBar` mirroring the existing timer reset trio.

## Context for implementers

- Tracking applies to **both** `TalkingPointItem` (blue bullets) and `ExampleItem` (yellow bullets in `InlineExamples` and `Examples`) — both are in `src/components/ContentBlock.jsx`.
- Path-based id construction: parent passes `path` prop; child appends its index. Top-level call from `TalkingPoints` starts with `${moduleId}::${sectionId}::${i}`. Children append `.${i}`. Examples can use suffix `:ex${i}`.
- Follow `TimerContext.jsx` exactly — same shape (provider, hook, localStorage key with `:v1`, reset funcs, no-op on SSR).
- `ModuleView` already wraps content in `PopupBudgetProvider`; `ProgressProvider` should wrap at the same level OR globally in `App.jsx`. Global is cleaner because `lastOpened` is a single course-wide pointer.
- Reset confirmations follow the exact `confirmAndRun` pattern in `TimerBar.jsx`.

## How This Plan Is Executed

Each phase is a self-contained, verifiable unit. `/execute-a-plan` reads this file, finds the first unchecked phase in the Progress section, implements it end-to-end, marks the phase `[x]` on completion, reports a summary, and asks two questions: (1) whether the result is acceptable and (2) whether to proceed to the next unchecked phase. If the user reports issues, the skill unchecks the box (`[x]` → `[ ]`) and addresses them within the same phase. If the user confirms AND asks to continue, the skill advances to the next phase — then stops and asks again. If the user confirms without asking to continue, the skill stops. A single invocation can chain through several phases this way, but there is always a full report and an explicit go-ahead between each.

## Phase Type Legend

Each phase below is marked with a **Type**:

- `AFK` (Away From Keyboard) — Claude can complete the phase autonomously end-to-end; all acceptance criteria are verifiable by automated tests or deterministic checks.
- `HITL` (Human In The Loop) — the phase needs human input before or during execution.

Default is `AFK`. Mark `HITL` only when human action is genuinely required.

## Phase 1: Manual covered toggle on every talking point

**Covers**: `ProgressContext` foundation, path-based ids, clickable bullets that toggle covered state, persistence across reloads.
**Type**: `AFK`

### What to build

- New `src/components/ProgressContext.jsx` with shape `{ covered: Record<pointId, true>, lastOpened: pointId | null }`. Provider, `useProgress` hook, `localStorage` key `lecturer-guide:progress:v1`. Operations: `toggleCovered(pointId)`, `setCovered(pointId, bool)`, `setLastOpened(pointId | null)`, `resetSection(sectionId)`, `resetModule(moduleId)`, `resetAll()`. Reset funcs scope by `pointId` prefix matching (`<moduleId>::<sectionId>::` for section, `<moduleId>::` for module).
- Wrap `App.jsx` content in `<ProgressProvider>` so `lastOpened` is course-wide.
- Thread `path` prop through `TalkingPoints` → `TalkingPointItem` (recursive) and through `Examples` / `InlineExamples` → `ExampleItem`. Need `moduleId` and `sectionId` available — pass from `ModuleView`/`SectionContent` down into `ContentBlock`. Update `ContentBlock` signature to accept `pathPrefix`.
- Bullet rendering: replace plain `●` span with a `<button>` that calls `toggleCovered(pointId)`. When `covered === true`: bullet becomes `✓` (same color family), text gets `opacity-50` + `line-through` (subtle, not aggressive). Hover always shows pointer cursor + tooltip "Маркирай като казано / отмаркирай".
- Apply identical treatment to `ExampleItem` yellow bullet.
- For `TalkingPointItem` with children but no `detail`: the toggle expand-arrow stays as-is; the bullet check lives separately on the same row before the arrow (small click target, doesn't conflict with expand).

### Integration points

- `WHEN App renders` — `src/App.jsx` — wrap children in `ProgressProvider` (similar to existing `TimerProvider`).
- `WHEN ContentBlock renders talking-points` — `src/components/ContentBlock.jsx` `TalkingPoints` and `TalkingPointItem` — accept `pathPrefix`, compute `pointId = ${pathPrefix}.${i}` per item, pass down recursively.
- `WHEN ContentBlock renders examples` — `Examples` and `InlineExamples` and `ExampleItem` in same file — accept `pathPrefix`, compute id with `:ex${i}` suffix.
- `WHEN ModuleView renders SectionContent` — `src/components/ModuleView.jsx` — pass `moduleId` and `sectionId` so `ContentBlock` can build the prefix.

### Acceptance criteria

- WHEN the user clicks a talking-point bullet, THE system SHALL toggle the `covered` state for that point.
- WHEN a talking point is `covered`, THE system SHALL render its bullet as `✓` and dim the text (opacity reduced, optional strike-through).
- WHEN the user clicks the bullet of an already-covered point, THE system SHALL revert the point to uncovered visual state.
- WHEN the user reloads the page, THE system SHALL restore all previously toggled `covered` states from `localStorage`.
- WHEN the user clicks an example bullet, THE system SHALL toggle that example's `covered` state independently from talking points.
- GIVEN no point has ever been toggled, WHEN the page first loads, THE system SHALL render every bullet in the original (uncovered) state.

---

## Phase 2: "Готово" button in popup + lastOpened accent

**Covers**: explicit confirmation button in `DetailPopup` that marks the source point covered AND records it as `lastOpened`; visual accent for the single most-recently-confirmed point.
**Type**: `AFK`

### What to build

- `DetailPopup` accepts a new prop `pointId: string | null`. When non-null, render a primary "✓ Готово" button in the popup header (right side, before the timer / X). Button click → `setCovered(pointId, true)` + `setLastOpened(pointId)` + `onClose()`.
- Plain close paths (X button, Esc key, backdrop click) remain unchanged — they DO NOT touch progress state. Keep current `onClose` semantics.
- Pass `pointId` from `TalkingPointItem` and `ExampleItem` into the `<DetailPopup>` they render.
- Visual for `lastOpened`: when a talking-point's `pointId === lastOpened`, render a 3px violet left border (`border-l-[3px] border-violet-400`) on the row + slight `bg-violet-50/40`. Single point in the whole course at any time. Same treatment for examples (using yellow-violet hybrid OR just same violet — pick one and stay consistent).
- "Готово" button styling: emerald primary (`bg-emerald-600 text-white hover:bg-emerald-700`), small, with `✓ Готово` label. Distinct from the X close.

### Integration points

- `WHEN DetailPopup renders header` — `src/components/DetailPopup.jsx` line 57-77 — add "Готово" button left of the timer span.
- `WHEN DetailPopup user presses Готово` — new handler in `DetailPopup.jsx` — call optional `onConfirm` prop OR consume `useProgress()` directly with passed `pointId`.
- `WHEN TalkingPointItem opens popup` — `src/components/ContentBlock.jsx` lines 47-53 and 99-105 — pass `pointId={pointId}` to `<DetailPopup>`.
- `WHEN ExampleItem opens popup` — `src/components/ContentBlock.jsx` lines 187-193 — pass `pointId={pointId}` to `<DetailPopup>`.
- `WHEN TalkingPointItem renders row` — same file — apply `lastOpened` accent classes when `pointId === lastOpened`.

### Acceptance criteria

- WHEN the user clicks "Готово" in a popup, THE system SHALL mark the source point as `covered` AND record its `pointId` as `lastOpened` AND close the popup.
- WHEN the user closes a popup via X button, Esc key, or backdrop click, THE system SHALL NOT modify `covered` or `lastOpened` state.
- WHEN exactly one point is `lastOpened`, THE system SHALL render that point with a violet left border and subtle violet background tint.
- WHEN a different popup is confirmed via "Готово", THE system SHALL move the `lastOpened` accent to the new point and remove it from the previous one.
- GIVEN a point is already `covered`, WHEN the user opens its popup and clicks "Готово", THE system SHALL keep it `covered` AND update `lastOpened` to that point.
- WHEN the user reloads the page, THE system SHALL restore the `lastOpened` accent on the same point.

---

## Phase 3: Reset controls in TimerBar

**Covers**: 3 reset buttons — section / module / all — for the progress state, mirroring the existing timer reset trio.
**Type**: `AFK`

### What to build

- Add a second row (or inline group on the right) in `TimerBar.jsx` with 3 buttons styled like the existing `resetBtn` but using a check-glyph: `✓ подт.`, `✓ модул`, `✓ всичко`. Different hover color from timer resets to distinguish (e.g. emerald hover instead of rose).
- Each click → `window.confirm` with Bulgarian confirmation text (mirror existing `confirmAndRun` pattern), then dispatch the matching `resetSection` / `resetModule` / `resetAll` from `useProgress()`.
- The `✓ подт.` button SHALL be hidden when no active section (single-section module) — same condition as timer's `⟲ подт.`.
- Reset semantics: clearing scope removes all matching `pointId`s from `covered` AND clears `lastOpened` if it falls inside the cleared scope.

### Integration points

- `WHEN TimerBar renders reset buttons` — `src/components/TimerBar.jsx` lines 220-264 — add a parallel group of 3 progress reset buttons.
- `WHEN user clicks a progress reset button` — same file — call `useProgress()` reset funcs guarded by `confirmAndRun`.

### Acceptance criteria

- WHEN the user clicks `✓ подт.` and confirms, THE system SHALL remove `covered` flags for every point under the active section AND clear `lastOpened` if it falls in that section.
- WHEN the user clicks `✓ модул` and confirms, THE system SHALL remove `covered` flags for every point under the active module AND clear `lastOpened` if it falls in that module.
- WHEN the user clicks `✓ всичко` and confirms, THE system SHALL remove all `covered` flags AND clear `lastOpened` to `null`.
- WHEN the user cancels any confirm dialog, THE system SHALL leave progress state unchanged.
- WHEN the active module has only one section, THE system SHALL hide the `✓ подт.` button.
- GIVEN reset succeeds, WHEN the user reloads the page, THE system SHALL persist the cleared state (no zombie covered marks reappear).

---

## Progress

- [x] Phase 1 — Manual covered toggle on every talking point
- [x] Phase 2 — "Готово" button in popup + lastOpened accent
- [x] Phase 3 — Reset controls in TimerBar

## Notes

- Path id construction edge case: a talking point with both `detail` and `children` (Module 1 has these). The point itself + each child get distinct ids; clicking parent bullet only marks parent, children are independent.
- Accessibility: bullet buttons need `aria-label` like `Маркирай като казано` / `Отмаркирай`.
- If lecturer-guide adds search/jump-to features later, `lastOpened` is the natural anchor for "go to where I was" — keep the API name semantic.
